markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Expose memory graph state to visual/debug surfaces with stable IDs and source citations. This MT belongs to MemoryGraphAndClaims. Implement the Handshake-native memory system translated from research into product contracts.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Paper-derived memory architecture must be expressed as Handshake-native MemoryOntology, MemoryFact, MemoryPassage, ClaimLifecycle, ConflictResolution, and EvidenceSpan contracts.
Probationary claims cannot become stable retrieval facts without evidence, conflict checks, and EventLedger promotion.
scope.expected_behavior: MemoryGraphVisualDebugPayload leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: MemoryGraphVisualDebugPayload is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

evidence: Built+verified ff67de1c; MemoryGraphAndClaims group: 21 memory-graph tests pass on real PostgreSQL (ontology/facts/conflict/bridge/projection/api/fixtures); migrations 0240-0243 with down+rollback test; facts back knowledge_claims (reuse lifecycle+conflict authority), projections marked authority_class=projection.

wp_validator_review_v2.review_method: Integration Validator multi-agent workflow (wf_0ac25815-485 + wf_98386044-70a): independent per-MT reviewer + adversarial confirm on FAIL. No ACP. Governance/repo paperwork non-blocking per operator directive (2026-06-18). Bar = good code vs MT contract + WP packet + current Master Spec v02.194, plus the WP_VALIDATOR Spec-Realism Gate.
wp_validator_review_v2.summary: MT-127 requires a typed backend MemoryGraphVisualDebugPayload exposing memory-graph state to visual/debug surfaces with stable IDs and source citations, as a projection over PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority. The code delivers a real builder composing the ontology/fact/passage-evidence projections plus claim-state/fact-label/open-conflict counts, marked authority_class="projection" with a schema_id, proven against real Handshake-managed PostgreSQL. Aligned with current Master Spec 10.20 / 2.3.13.11.
findings: visual_debug.rs:55-69 defines MemoryGraphVisualDebugPayload with workspace_id, schema_id, three subgraph projections, claim_state_counts, fact_label_counts, open_conflict_count, and authority_class — exactly the structured visual-debug state Master Spec 10.20 (10-product-surfaces.md:8546) and 2.3.13.11 require, as a projection (never a second store, spec line 8548).

visual_debug.rs:76-102 build_memory_graph_visual_debug composes the REAL projection builders (build_ontology_graph/build_fact_graph/build_passage_evidence_graph from projection.rs:68/160/290, confirmed non-stub bodies) plus live SQL aggregate queries against knowledge_claims (105-130), knowledge_memory_facts (133-161), and knowledge_claim_conflicts (165-178). schema_id and authority_class='projection' are hard-set.

sub_rule_1: grep of visual_debug.rs for todo!/unimplemented!/unreachable!/Unavailable/stub/fake/mock/'deferred'/'pending MT'/'not yet wired' returned NONE; no deferred-live escape on the required path.

sub_rule_2: proof touches REAL Handshake-managed PostgreSQL. tests/knowledge_memory_projection_tests.rs:556-638 visual_debug_payload_composes_graphs_and_counts builds a fixture via MemoryFixture::setup -> knowledge_pg_support.rs:51 ManagedPostgres::ensure_running (the Handshake-native managed PG, real migrations via db.run_migrations at line 156), inserts real ontology term/fact/passage, then asserts payload.schema_id, authority_class='projection', subgraph populations, claim_state_counts.proposed==1, fact_label_counts.source==1, open_conflict_count==0. Not an implementer-authored in-memory mock.
sub_rule_3: MT lifecycle.claimed_by=null, completed_by=null, status READY_FOR_VALIDATION — no self-certified COMPLETED transition; sub-rule NA.

Commit ff67de1c touches only src/ and tests/ (all within packet allowed_paths); api/mod.rs change is a single additive merge line; no /.GOV/ or governance boundary touch. No forbidden tech (SQLite/Docker/external search/graph services) present.

Aside (non-blocking): MT evidence text cites migrations 0240-0243 and 21 memory-graph tests for the group; this MT's own committed proof is the 1 visual-debug builder test, which is sufficient and real for the MT-127 surface.

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
